message.content: Adversaries may exploit user execution of malicious images to facilitate unauthorized code execution. This technique involves backdooring images on platforms like AWS, GCP, Azure, or container runtimes such as Docker. These compromised images can be uploaded to public repositories, where unsuspecting users may download and deploy them, inadvertently executing malicious code. This can lead to activities such as cryptocurrency mining within the instance or container. Adversaries often name these images to mimic legitimate ones, increasing the likelihood of user deployment. Notably, the threat group TeamTNT has utilized this method with malicious Docker images. Mitigation strategies include verifying the integrity and source of images before deployment, using trusted repositories, and employing runtime security tools to detect and prevent the execution of malicious code.
